evidence.description: Scenario 1 ties quote fetch to holdings entry, while REQ-007 ties quote fetch to ticker selection. Mixed-currency behavior is also only specified for the successful usdJpy-available path.
REQ-007 requires quote fetch on ticker selection and JPY normalization for mixed-currency holdings, but it does not define the usdJpy-missing behavior assumed by downstream artifacts.
The spec explicitly covers missing-input, mixed-currency, and quote-failure edge cases for the portfolio panel.
The declared utility contract separates status states and aggregate calculation responsibilities cleanly, with no contradiction beyond the cited spec-fidelity findings.
The spec keeps a clear purity boundary between the React Query hook, the DOM panel, and the pure portfolio aggregation utility.
Proof obligations cover normalization, missing-input exclusion, pending-rate, and ready-state totals only; they omit quote-fetch trigger, native-currency display, and quote-error preservation.
Planned tests cover normalization, summary display, and quote-query behavior, but do not define assertions for native-price display or the quote-failure UI contract.
